title: 10 best herbal creams for hypertension

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with hypertension because they contain compounds that have been shown to help relax blood vessels and lower blood pressure. The glycyrrhizin present in licorice root has a natural ability to reduce inflammation, which can contribute to the development of hypertension. Additionally, the flavonoids found in licorice cream may also help to improve blood flow and reduce vascular resistance, further supporting its potential benefits for individuals with high blood pressure.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has been shown to help lower blood pressure by inhibiting the activity of enzymes that constrict blood vessels, reducing peripheral resistance and improving circulation.
Licoricidin: This compound has been found to have a vasodilatory effect, which means it helps relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, both of which can help reduce blood pressure.

Flavonoids: Herbal licorice cream contains flavonoids like glabridin and isoliquiritigenin, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help protect against the damage caused by high blood pressure and improve overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of hypertension.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes (underground stems) of the licorice plant are rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to improve cardiovascular health and help manage hypertension.
remedy_recipe: Extract 10 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots using 100 ml of ethanol at room temperature for 24 hours.
Filter the extracted mixture through cheesecloth and collect the filtrate, discarding the solids after 30 minutes.
Evaporate the filtered liquid to reduce its volume by half, resulting in a concentrated glycyrrhizin-rich extract within 90 minutes.
Mix the concentrate with 50 grams of petroleum jelly, 20 grams of beeswax, and 10 ml of distilled water to create the cream base over 15 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized containers and allow it to cool and set at room temperature for 2 hours before use within a week.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with hypertension because they contain valerenic acid, a natural compound that has been shown to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow. This relaxation of blood vessels reduces blood pressure, alleviating the symptoms of hypertension. Additionally, valerian's sedative properties can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for high blood pressure. By addressing these underlying factors, herbal valerian creams offer a natural and effective way to manage hypertension and promote overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Relaxation and calming effects: Valerian's sedative properties help to calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can contribute to high blood pressure.
Vasodilation: Valerian's ability to dilate blood vessels helps to improve blood flow and lower blood pressure by allowing blood to flow more easily through the veins and arteries.
Antioxidant properties: Valerian's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to high blood pressure and cardiovascular disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain alkaloids such as valepotriates, which have been shown to help lower blood pressure and reduce symptoms of hypertension.
Rhyzomes: Valerian rhyzomes have a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have been found to relax blood vessels and improve circulation, making them useful for hypertension management.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried valerian root powder by grinding 10g of high-quality dried valeriana officinalis root into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Combine the valerian root powder with 20ml of cold-pressed sweet almond oil and 5ml of beeswax in a double boiler, heating it at 60°c for 15 minutes.
Add 10 drops of vitamin e oil and 5ml of aloe vera gel to the mixture, stirring until well combined and allowing it to cool to 40°c.
Transfer the cooled mixture to a sterilized glass jar and store it in the refrigerator at 2-8°c for a minimum of 48 hours before use, shaking the jar daily.
After the infusion period, use a spatula to scoop out the required amount of cream, applying it topically to the affected area twice daily as needed, or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with hypertension because of its calming properties, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety levels. High blood pressure is often linked to emotional factors, and chamomile's natural tranquilizers work to soothe the mind and body. Additionally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds may also help to dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and reducing blood pressure. Regular use of herbal chamomile creams can promote relaxation, ease tension, and provide a natural approach to managing hypertension.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce blood pressure by decreasing inflammation in blood vessels, which can contribute to high blood pressure.
Calming and sedative effects: The soothing and calming properties of chamomile help relax the body and mind, reducing stress and anxiety, both of which are common triggers for hypertension.
Vasodilation and relaxation: Chamomile's flavonoids and terpenoids cause blood vessels to dilate, or widen, which can lower blood pressure by allowing blood to flow more freely through the vessels.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin, a flavonoid that helps relax blood vessels and reduce blood pressure.
Flowers: Contain bisabolol, an anti-inflammatory compound that soothes blood vessels and improves circulation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the ground matricaria chamomilla powder with sweet almond oil and beeswax in a double boiler at 160°f for 30 minutes.
Add aloe vera gel and vitamin e oil to the mixture and stir well, allowing it to infuse for 10 hours overnight.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding any solids and adjusting the consistency with more sweet almond oil as needed.
Pour the final cream into sterilized jars and store them in the refrigerator at 40°f to ensure freshness for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort creams helps with hypertension because they contain hyperforin, a bioactive compound that has been shown to exhibit vasodilatory properties. This means it can help relax blood vessels and improve circulation, reducing the pressure on arterial walls and subsequently lowering blood pressure. Additionally, hyperforin has anti-inflammatory effects, which may also contribute to its ability to alleviate hypertension by reducing inflammation in the blood vessels. By targeting both blood vessel relaxation and inflammation, St John's wort creams may provide a natural and effective way to manage hypertension.
remedy_properties: Hypericin: This active compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce blood pressure by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels.
Hypotensive flavonoids: Flavonoids present in St. John's Wort have been shown to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, which can help lower blood pressure.

Adaptogenic terpenes: Terpenes such as limonene and sesquiterpene lactones in St. John's Wort have an adaptogenic effect, helping the body adapt to stress and reducing anxiety, a common comorbidity with hypertension.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to lower blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels and improving circulation.
Flowers: Containing hypericin and pseudohypericin, these compounds have been shown to reduce blood pressure by inhibiting the activity of certain enzymes that regulate blood vessel constriction.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried hypericum perforatum flowers in quantities of 20-30 grams.
Dry the flowers in a warm, well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried hypericum perforatum flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered herb with sweet almond oil or coconut oil in a ratio of 1:2 by weight, stirring well.
Transfer the mixture to sterilized glass jars and store at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with hypertension because they contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to help reduce blood pressure by dilating blood vessels and improving blood flow. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also help to relax blood vessels, which can further contribute to lowering blood pressure. Additionally, the cream's potassium content can help counteract the effects of sodium in the body, promoting healthy blood pressure levels.
remedy_properties: Potassium content: Dandelion cream contains high levels of potassium, which can help counterbalance the effects of sodium and reduce blood pressure by promoting healthy blood vessel function.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in dandelion cream have been shown to improve blood flow, relax blood vessels, and decrease inflammation, all of which can contribute to a reduction in blood pressure.

Triterpenes: Triterpenes, such as taraxasterol, found in dandelion cream, have been demonstrated to exhibit anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory effects, which can help reduce blood pressure by improving blood vessel elasticity and reducing resistance.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in potassium, which helps to lower blood pressure by counteracting the effects of sodium and promoting healthy blood vessel function.
Seeds: Contain triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to have antihypertensive properties, helping to relax blood vessels and reduce blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried taraxacum officinale leaves, 50g of beeswax, and 20ml of coconut oil.
Combine the dried taraxacum officinale leaves with 200ml of hot water to create a decoction, steeping for 30 minutes.
Strain the decoction through cheesecloth, discarding the solids, then add 50g of glycerin and mix well.
Melt the beeswax in a double boiler, heating until it reaches 80°c, before slowly adding the taraxacum officinale infusion mixture.
Continue to heat the mixture for an additional 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a smooth consistency suitable for application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with hypertension because of their unique ability to relax blood vessels, improve circulation, and reduce inflammation. The anti-inflammatory properties in aloe vera help to ease tension in the blood vessels, allowing blood to flow more freely and reducing pressure on the cardiovascular system. Additionally, aloe vera's ability to promote healthy digestion and detoxification can also contribute to its positive effects on hypertension by reducing stress on the body and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Potassium content: Herbal aloe creams contain high amounts of potassium, which helps to counterbalance the effects of sodium and reduce blood pressure by promoting healthy blood vessel function.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the body, which is often linked to hypertension, and also improve overall cardiovascular health.
Antioxidant activity: Herbal aloe creams contain antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to high blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Aloe roots contain bioactive compounds that help regulate blood pressure and alleviate symptoms associated with hypertension.
Bark: The bark of the aloe plant contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit hypotensive properties and reduce blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Extract fresh aloe vera gel from mature leaves using scissors or a sharp knife within 30 minutes of harvesting.
Mix 250 grams of aloe vera gel with 100 grams of coconut oil in a stainless steel bowl for 15 minutes.
Add 50 grams of dried ashwagandha powder and stir counterclockwise for 5 minutes to combine ingredients smoothly.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 20 minutes or until it reaches 60-70°c, then remove from heat and let cool completely.
Strain the cooled mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar, discarding solids and storing in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with hypertension because they contain a unique blend of herbs that work synergistically to help regulate blood pressure. The extracts from the maidenhair tree, also known as Ginkgo biloba, have been shown to dilate blood vessels and improve blood flow, reducing the strain on the heart and lowering blood pressure. Additionally, the creams often include other herbal ingredients such as hawthorn and lycopene, which help to relax blood vessels and reduce inflammation, further contributing to their hypotensive effects.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Ginkgo biloba extracts contain flavonoids, which have been shown to improve blood vessel function and reduce blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels and improving vascular reactivity.

Terpenoids: Terpenoids in Ginkgo biloba have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to hypertension.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids present in Maidenhair Tree extracts have been shown to have a hypotensive effect by inhibiting the activity of enzymes involved in blood vessel constriction, leading to decreased blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bioactive compounds, which help to regulate blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of hypertension.
Barks: The barks contain tannins, flavonoids, and alkaloids that have been traditionally used to reduce blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including ginkgo biloba extract 20% concentration 100ml, coconut oil 50g, beeswax 10g, and vitamin e oil 5 drops.
Combine the ginkgo biloba extract with coconut oil in a heat-proof glass bowl and stir until well mixed taking approximately 10 minutes.
Add the beeswax to the mixture and stir until it melts completely taking around 15-20 minutes over low heat.
Remove the mixture from heat and add vitamin e oil stirring well for an additional 5 minutes allowing it to cool slightly.
Pour the final cream into sterile jars filling them to the top and seal immediately allowing it to set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi creams helps with hypertension because they contain extracts from the herb Bacopa monnieri, which has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to promote relaxation and reduce stress. The cream's active ingredients help to dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow and lowering blood pressure. Additionally, brahmi's adaptogenic properties enable it to regulate the body's response to stress, further contributing to its hypotensive effects. By reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, brahmi creams provide a natural and effective solution for managing hypertension.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Brahmi's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to hypertension by damaging blood vessels and increasing blood pressure.

Vasodilation-promoting compounds: Brahmi contains compounds like terpenoids and glycosides that help relax blood vessels, improve blood flow, and lower blood pressure by causing vasodilation (widening of the blood vessels).

Adaptogenic and stress-reducing properties: Brahmi's adaptogenic properties help the body adapt to stress, reducing the negative impact of stress on blood pressure and promoting overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in alkaloids and saponins, which help to reduce blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels and improving cardiovascular health.
Rhyzomes: Contain bioactive compounds like flavonoids and terpenes, which have been shown to exhibit hypotensive properties and help manage hypertension.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 250 grams of high-quality dried bacopa monnieri leaves and stems.
Mix the bacopa powder with 100 milliliters of distilled water in a clean glass bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for exactly 10 minutes to activate the herbal compounds.
Strain the warm liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar leaving behind the solids.
Combine the filtered liquid with 50 grams of melted coconut oil and stir well.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with hypertension because of its unique ability to reduce blood pressure by inhibiting the formation of pro-inflammatory compounds in the body. The active compound curcumin in turmeric has been shown to relax and dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing resistance in the arteries. Additionally, curcumin also helps to regulate blood vessel function by reducing the activity of enzymes that promote vasoconstriction, leading to a decrease in blood pressure and improved cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Curcumin, a bioactive compound found in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help reduce blood pressure by preventing the constriction of blood vessels and improving vascular function.
Potassium: Turmeric cream contains potassium, an essential mineral that helps regulate blood pressure by counteracting the effects of sodium and promoting healthy vasodilation.

Vasodilatory compounds: The combination of curcumin and other bioactive compounds in turmeric cream helps to dilate blood vessels, reducing blood pressure and improving overall cardiovascular health by increasing peripheral resistance and decreasing cardiac workload.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in curcuminoids, which have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of hypertension.
Roots: Known for their ability to regulate blood flow and improve cardiovascular health, making them an effective component in reducing hypertension.
remedy_recipe: Grind 50 grams of dried turmeric root into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 2 minutes.
Mix 20 grams of coconut oil with 10 grams of beeswax in a double boiler, heating until melted and smooth over 5 minutes.
Combine the ground turmeric powder with 5 grams of vitamin e oil and 2 grams of glycerin in a small bowl, stirring well for 1 minute.
Gradually add the powder mixture to the coconut oil and beeswax mixture while continuously stirring for an additional 3 minutes until fully incorporated.
Pour the cream mixture into sterilized glass jars and let cool at room temperature or refrigerate for 24 hours before use for optimal efficacy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with hypertension because they contain saponins, which have been shown to help lower blood pressure by reducing inflammation and improving blood vessel function. The creams also possess anti-oxidant properties that help protect against oxidative stress, a common contributor to high blood pressure. Additionally, the soothing and calming effects of marshmallow creams can help reduce anxiety and stress, which are often linked to hypertension. By addressing these underlying factors, herbal marshmallow creams may provide natural relief from hypertension symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Herbal marshmallow creams contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to high blood pressure by damaging blood vessels and increasing vascular resistance. This property helps to mitigate these effects and promote healthy blood vessel function.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in herbal marshmallow creams, such as flavonoids and saponins, can help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and cardiovascular system, which is a major risk factor for hypertension. By decreasing inflammation, these compounds may help to lower blood pressure.

Potassium-rich properties: Marshmallow roots are a natural source of potassium, an essential mineral that helps regulate fluid balance and blood pressure. Consuming herbal marshmallow creams rich in potassium can help counterbalance the effects of sodium in the body and reduce blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the marshmallow plant contain mucilages that help reduce blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of hypertension.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the marshmallow plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help relax blood vessels, reducing blood pressure and alleviating hypertension symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh marshmallow root (althaea officinalis) leaves and dry them in a warm oven at 60°c for 2 hours.
Combine 50 grams of dried marshmallow root powder with 20 grams of glycerin, 10 grams of beeswax, and 200 ml of distilled water to create the herbal extract.
Steep the herbal mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes at 60°c, then strain it through a cheesecloth into a clean container.
Combine the filtered herbal extract with 50 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of shea butter, mixing until a smooth consistency is achieved.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for 1 hour before transferring it to an airtight container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with hypertension because they contain the herb Echinacea, which has been shown to help regulate blood pressure. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also contribute to its ability to reduce blood pressure by soothing and calming blood vessels, allowing them to relax and dilate. Additionally, Kansas coneflower creams have been found to increase the body's production of nitric oxide, a compound that helps to relax and widen blood vessels, further reducing blood pressure and promoting overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids and phenolic acids: These bioactive compounds have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and vasodilatory effects, which may help reduce blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels and improving circulation.

Saponins: These natural compounds have been found to lower blood pressure in animal studies by reducing peripheral resistance and improving endothelial function, potentially leading to better cardiovascular health.

Triterpenoids: Kan-sas coneflower contains a group of triterpenoids called echinocystic acids, which may help regulate blood pressure by inhibiting the activity of certain enzymes involved in hypertension.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help regulate blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of hypertension.
Barks: Contains flavonoids, which have been shown to have antioxidant properties that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hypertension.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry high-quality echinacea angustifolia roots for the desired dosage of 30 grams per serving.
Combine the dried roots with 100ml of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil in a heat-resistant glass container.
Heat the mixture at 60°c to 70°c for 1 hour and 15 minutes while stirring occasionally until the infusion is complete.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into another glass container and discard the solids after 30 minutes of preparation time.
Allow the herbal cream to mature at room temperature for 24 hours before packaging in airtight containers with a shelf life of 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn creams helps with hypertension because they contain flavonoids, oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs), and other bioactive compounds that work synergistically to improve cardiovascular health. The creams help to relax blood vessels, increasing blood flow and reducing peripheral resistance, which in turn lowers blood pressure. Additionally, the flavonoids have antioxidant properties that protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, further contributing to their blood-pressure-lowering effects. As a result, herbal hawthorn creams can be a valuable adjunct therapy for individuals managing hypertension.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Hawthorn cream's flavonoids, such as quercetin and epicatechin, have been shown to improve blood vessel function by increasing nitric oxide production, which helps to relax and dilate blood vessels, reducing blood pressure.

Oligomeric Proanthocyanidins (OPCs): OPCs in hawthorn cream help to protect the lining of blood vessels from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to hypertension, while also improving blood flow and lowering blood pressure.

Cardiac glycosides: Hawthorn's cardiac glycosides, such as digoxin, have a positive inotropic effect on the heart, meaning they help increase its pumping efficiency, reducing the workload on the heart and lowering blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs), which help to reduce blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health.
Barks: Contain bioactive compounds like quercetin, kaempferol, and epicatechin, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties beneficial for hypertension management.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh crataegus monogyna leaves and stems in the morning, ensuring they are clean and free from any contaminants.
Dry the collected plant material using shade drying or air circulation for at least 24 hours to reduce moisture content.
Combine one part dried crataegus monogyna with two parts carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil, infusing the mixture for 4-6 weeks under cold conditions.
Strain and filter the infused mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, discarding any solids and transferring the liquid to a clean glass container.

Mix one part of the filtered herbal extract with two parts emulsifier such as beeswax or cetyl alcohol, heating gently until smooth and creamy, then allowing it to cool and thicken for 30 minutes before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese peony creams helps with hypertension because they contain potent herbal extracts that help to relax blood vessels, improve circulation, and reduce stress on the cardiovascular system. The creamy formula provides a soothing and calming effect, which can help to alleviate symptoms of high blood pressure such as anxiety and fatigue. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of Chinese peony creams may also contribute to their blood pressure-lowering effects by reducing inflammation in the body's vascular tissues.
remedy_properties: Diuretic Properties: The herbs in Chinese peony creams, such as Danshen (Salvia miltiorrhiza) and Gan Cao (Glycyrrhizae uraeensis), increase urine production to remove excess sodium and water from the body, helping to reduce blood pressure.

Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The antioxidants in Chinese peony creams, such as Baicalin (Scutellaria baicalensis) and Peony root extract, help to protect blood vessels from damage caused by free radicals and inflammation, which can contribute to hypertension.
Vasodilatory Properties: The flavonoids present in Chinese peony creams, such as Quercetin and Kaempferol, relax blood vessel walls, allowing for better blood flow and reducing blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which help to relax blood vessels and lower blood pressure.
Rhyzomes: Contain starch and cellulose, which can help to reduce blood lipid levels and improve blood circulation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh paeonia lactiflora roots weighing 250 grams and clean them thoroughly.
Dry the cleaned paeonia lactiflora roots in a warm oven at 60°c for 3 hours then store them in air-tight containers.
Grind 100 grams of dried paeonia lactiflora root into fine powder using an electric grinder for 30 seconds.
Combine the powdered paeonia lactiflora with 200 milliliters of cold distilled water and let it steep for 2 hours at room temperature.
Mix 50 grams of beeswax, 25 grams of coconut oil, and the filtered paeonia lactiflora infusion in a heat-resistant glass bowl over low heat for 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Selfheal creams helps with hypertension because they contain the herb Prunella vulgaris, which has been shown to have a significant impact on blood pressure levels. The cream's active compounds help to relax blood vessels and improve circulation, reducing the strain on the cardiovascular system and lowering blood pressure. Additionally, selfheal's antioxidant properties help to protect against oxidative stress, which is often associated with hypertension. By addressing these underlying factors, herbal selfheal creams can provide a natural and effective approach to managing high blood pressure.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Selfheal cream's antioxidant compounds help protect the blood vessels from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to high blood pressure.
Vasodilatory Effects: The cream's flavonoids and terpenes have been shown to relax blood vessel walls, improving blood flow and reducing blood pressure.

Anti-inflammatory Activity: Selfheal cream's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, which can contribute to hypertension by causing vascular constriction and increasing peripheral resistance.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the selfheal plant are used to make medicinal creams for hypertension due to their ability to reduce blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of high blood pressure.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, the underground stems of the plant, contain compounds that help relax blood vessels, reducing blood pressure and alleviating hypertension symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh prunella vulgaris leaves at dawn and wash them thoroughly with distilled water.
Dry the leaves in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 6 hours or use a food dehydrator.
Grind 100g of dried prunella vulgaris leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered herb with 200ml of distilled water and simmer for 30 minutes in a double boiler.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth and combine it with 50g of beeswax and 10ml of coconut oil to create a creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with hypertension because they contain compounds that have a calming effect on the body, reducing stress and anxiety levels. Lavender's natural sedative properties help to slow down heart rate and blood pressure, promoting relaxation and vasodilation. Additionally, lavender's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which are common underlying factors of hypertension. As a result, using English lavender creams can help to naturally lower blood pressure and alleviate hypertension symptoms.
remedy_properties: Lavender Oil's Calcium Channel Blocker Activity: Lavender oil has been shown to have a calcium channel blocker effect, which can help relax blood vessels and lower blood pressure, making it easier for the heart to pump blood.

Antioxidant Properties: English lavender contains antioxidants that help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, which can contribute to hypertension. By reducing oxidative stress, lavender cream may help improve cardiovascular health and reduce blood pressure.

Anxiolytic and Sedative Effects: Lavender's calming effects on the nervous system can help reduce anxiety and stress, common triggers of hypertension. By promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety levels, herbal English lavender creams may indirectly help lower blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health.
Seeds: Containing sesquiterpenes and terpenoids that have been shown to relax blood vessels and improve circulation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality lavandula angustifolia flowers, drying them for 2 weeks in a warm, well-ventilated area.
Infuse dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil at a ratio of 1:4.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes to allow for optimal extraction of bioactive compounds.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, discarding solids and retaining liquid for use in cream production.
Whip together infused oil, beeswax, vitamin e oil, and shea butter in a double boiler until smooth and creamy, taking approximately 10-15 minutes to prepare.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense creams helps with hypertension because they contain boswellic acids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation in blood vessels and improve circulation. This can help to lower blood pressure by reducing resistance in the arteries and allowing blood to flow more freely. Additionally, frankincense has been found to have a vasodilatory effect, causing blood vessels to dilate and further reducing blood pressure. By addressing the root causes of hypertension, frankincense creams may provide a natural and effective way to manage high blood pressure and promote overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Frankincense cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce blood pressure by reducing inflammation in the blood vessels, which can constrict and increase blood pressure.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of frankincense cream can help protect against oxidative stress and damage to blood vessel walls, which can contribute to hypertension.
Vasodilatory: Frankincense cream's vasodilatory properties can help relax and widen blood vessels, reducing blood pressure by allowing blood to flow more freely through the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: Frankincense roots have been traditionally used to help lower blood pressure and reduce stress, making them a key ingredient in creams for hypertension relief.
Barks: The barks of the frankincense tree contain boswellic acids, which have been shown to inhibit platelet aggregation, reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease and hypertension.
remedy_recipe: Harvest boswellia serrata gum resin from mature trees for optimal bioactive compound content.
Dry the harvested boswellia serrata gum resin in a cool dark place for 14 days to reduce moisture levels.
Grind the dried boswellia serrata gum resin into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for efficient mixing.
Mix 250mg of ground boswellia serrata with 10g of coconut oil and 5g of beeswax in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat stir well and pour it into sterilized containers for storage and later application.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dong quai creams helps with hypertension because they contain compounds that have a positive impact on blood vessels and cardiovascular health. The cream's active ingredients, such as ferulic acid and lignans, help to relax constricted blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing pressure on the heart. Additionally, dong quai has been shown to inhibit the production of angiotensin II, a hormone that constricts blood vessels and increases blood pressure. By addressing these underlying causes of hypertension, herbal dong quai creams can help to alleviate symptoms and promote overall cardiovascular well-being.
remedy_properties: Diosgenin: This saponin compound has been shown to relax blood vessels, thereby reducing blood pressure and improving blood flow, which can be beneficial for individuals with hypertension.

Ligustilide: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and improve blood flow, thereby contributing to a decrease in blood pressure.

Coumarin: This compound has anticoagulant properties, which can help to prevent the formation of blood clots and reduce blood viscosity, both of which are important factors in hypertension management.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots of the dong quai plant help to reduce blood pressure and alleviate hypertension symptoms.
Barks: The barks of the dong quai plant contain flavonoids and saponins, which have been shown to exhibit vasodilatory effects, helping to lower blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried angelica sinensis roots and grind them into fine powder using mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Combine 20 grams of ground angelica sinensis with 10 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 1 hour, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and dissolved.
Remove from heat and add 2 tablespoons of sweet almond oil and 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil; mix well and let cool for 30 minutes.
Pour the cooled cream into sterilized jars, seal tightly, and store in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for hypertension are topical ointments or lotions infused with natural herbs and botanicals that help to reduce blood pressure levels. These creams work by relaxing blood vessels, improving circulation, and reducing stress and anxiety, all of which contribute to the development of hypertension. For example, creams containing lavender, chamomile, and passionflower have been shown to help lower blood pressure and improve sleep quality, leading to improved overall health and well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat hypertension with herbal creams is to opt for natural ingredients like hawthorn, garlic, and ginger. Apply topical creams containing these herbs to affected areas, such as the neck, shoulders, and chest, to help lower blood pressure. Look for products that also incorporate essential oils like lavender and peppermint to promote relaxation and reduce stress-induced hypertension. Always consult a healthcare professional before starting any new treatment regimen.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hypertension that are treated with herbal creams are peripheral artery disease, varicose veins, and Raynaud's disease. Herbal creams containing ingredients like ginkgo biloba, butcher's broom, and horse chestnut can help alleviate symptoms such as poor circulation, swelling, and pain. These natural remedies work by improving blood flow, reducing inflammation, and strengthening vascular walls to promote overall cardiovascular health.
